dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 1320 RH: Modern Worker Security Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Kiley of California (for himself and Mr. Messmer) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce
Additional sponsors: Mr. Kean, Mr. Carter of Georgia, Mr. Owens, Mr. Onder, Mr. Grothman, Mr. Baumgartner, Ms. Stefanik, and Mrs. Miller of Illinois
Reported with amendments, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on February 13, 2025

form.official-title: To ensure that the provision of portable benefits to an individual is not considered in determining whether such individual is an employee of a person.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Modern Worker Security Act.

subsection.text: Beginning on the date of enactment of this Act, for the purposes of any Federal law, a determination of whether an individual is an employee of a person shall be made without considering whether such person provides a benefit to the individual.
In this Act, the term benefit includes—

paragraph.text: a benefit, including a protection, that is provided to an individual for work performed for another person that the individual may maintain without regard to whether the individual continues to perform work for such person;

a benefit, including a protection, that is commonly provided to a full-time employee (such as workers’ compensation, skills training, professional development, paid leave, disability coverage, health insurance coverage, retirement savings, and short-term savings); and
a contribution, financial or otherwise, with respect to a benefit described in paragraph (1) or (2) that is—

subparagraph.text: made on behalf of an individual by a person in connection with work performed by the individual for the person;
made through a combination of subparagraphs (A) and (B).

bill.official-title-amendment: Amend the title so as to read: A bill to prohibit the consideration of benefits when determining whether an individual is an employee of a person..

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with amendments, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
